- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

5	1. Cluster evidence by theme
6	2. Plan report outline

10	"""

74	# Map-reduce clustering config (LAW VI)
75	PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_SIZE = int(os.getenv("PG_CLUSTER_BATCH_SIZE", "100"))
76	PG_CLUSTER_MAX_THEMES_BEFORE_MERGE = int(os.getenv("PG_CLUSTER_MAX_THEMES_BEFORE_MERGE", "20"))

79	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	# W3.11 — Single source of truth for faithfulness
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	# Prior bug: state.faithfulness_score and quality_metrics.faithfulness_score
83	# could diverge (e.g. -1.0 sentinel in metrics dict while state held 1.0).
84	# Rule: whenever a caller needs a faithfulness number, call this helper with
85	# the current claims list. Never read the stale state field directly for
86	# computation. The state field is only a display/propagation cache.
87	
88	def compute_faithfulness(claims: list[dict]) -> float:
89	    """Canonical faithfulness = faithful / scorable claims.
90	
91	    Excludes api_error claims (verification failed ≠ unfaithful).
92	    Returns 0.0 when no scorable claims exist (not the -1.0 sentinel, to avoid
93	    downstream confusion between "not computed" and "no claims").
94	    """
95	    if not claims:
96	        return 0.0
97	    scorable = [c for c in claims if c.get("verification_method") != "api_error"]
98	    if not scorable:
99	        return 0.0
100	    faithful = sum(1 for c in scorable if c.get("is_faithful"))
101	    return faithful / len(scorable)
102	
103	
104	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
105	# Short ID Remapping (Prosus research: 95.6% token reduction, <5% error rate)
106	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
107	
108	def _remap_evidence_ids(
109	    evidence: list[dict],
110	) -> tuple[list[dict], dict[str, str]]:
111	    """Remap ev_xxx IDs to sequential integers for LLM token efficiency.
112	
113	    Returns:
114	        (remapped_evidence, reverse_map) where:
115	        - remapped_evidence: copy of evidence with short integer IDs
116	        - reverse_map: {"1": "ev_abc123", "2": "ev_def456", ...}
117	    """
118	    forward_map: dict[str, str] = {}  # ev_xxx -> "1"
119	    reverse_map: dict[str, str] = {}  # "1" -> ev_xxx
120	
121	    for idx, ev in enumerate(evidence, start=1):
122	        original_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
123	        short_id = str(idx)
124	        forward_map[original_id] = short_id
125	        reverse_map[short_id] = original_id
126	
127	    remapped = []
128	    for ev in evidence:
129	        remapped_ev = dict(ev)
130	        original_id = remapped_ev.get("evidence_id", "")
131	        remapped_ev["evidence_id"] = forward_map.get(original_id, original_id)
132	        remapped.append(remapped_ev)
133	
134	    logger.debug(
135	        "[polaris graph] ID remap: %d evidence IDs remapped "
136	        "(~%d tokens saved, ~%.0f%% reduction)",
137	        len(forward_map),
138	        len(forward_map) * 4,  # ~5 tokens/UUID -> ~1 token/int = ~4 saved
139	        (1 - 1 / max(5, 1)) * 100,
140	    )
141	
142	    return remapped, reverse_map
143	
144	
145	def _reverse_remap_ids(
146	    short_ids: list[str],
147	    reverse_map: dict[str, str],
148	) -> list[str]:
149	    """Reverse-map short integer IDs back to original ev_xxx IDs.
150	
151	    Any IDs not found in reverse_map are logged and dropped.
152	    """
153	    restored: list[str] = []
154	    missing_count = 0
155	
156	    for sid in short_ids:
157	        original = reverse_map.get(str(sid))
158	        if original:
159	            restored.append(original)
160	        else:
161	            missing_count += 1
162	
163	    if missing_count:
164	        logger.warning(
165	            "[polaris graph] Reverse remap: %d/%d short IDs not found in map "
166	            "(LLM hallucinated IDs)",
167	            missing_count,
168	            len(short_ids),
169	        )
170	
171	    return restored
172	
173	
174	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
175	# FIX-E: Global Evidence Assignment (Two-Pass Synthesis)
176	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
177	
178	
179	async def _assign_evidence_globally(
180	    client: OpenRouterClient,
181	    outline_sections: list,
182	    evidence: list[dict],
183	) -> tuple[dict[str, list[str]], list[str]]:
184	    """FIX-E Pass 1: Globally assign evidence to sections via LLM.
185	
186	    Uses short-ID remapping for token efficiency (Prosus: 95.6% reduction).
187	    The LLM sees ALL evidence summaries and assigns each to its primary
188	    section. Evidence relevant to multiple sections is flagged as cross-section.
189	
190	    Args:
191	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
192	        outline_sections: List of SectionOutlineItem (or dicts with section_id, title, description).
193	        evidence: Full evidence pool (dicts with evidence_id, statement, source_url).
194	
195	    Returns:
196	        (section_assignments, cross_section_ids) where:
197	        - section_assignments: {section_id: [ev_xxx, ...]} primary assignments
198	        - cross_section_ids: [ev_xxx, ...] evidence visible to all sections
199	    """
200	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import GlobalEvidenceAssignment
201	
202	    if not evidence or not outline_sections:
203	        logger.warning("[polaris graph] FIX-E: Empty evidence or outline, skipping global assignment")
204	        return {}, []
205	
206	    # Short-ID remap for token efficiency
207	    remapped, reverse_map = _remap_evidence_ids(evidence)
208	
209	    # Build compact evidence summary: "ID: statement (source)"
210	    ev_lines = []
211	    for ev in remapped:
212	        short_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "?")
213	        stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:200]
214	        src = ev.get("source_url", "")
215	        # Extract domain from URL for brevity
216	        domain = src.split("//")[-1].split("/")[0] if "//" in src else src[:40]
217	        ev_lines.append(f"{short_id}: {stmt} [{domain}]")
218	
219	    evidence_block = "\n".join(ev_lines)
220	
221	    # Build section summary
222	    section_lines = []
223	    for sec in outline_sections:
224	        sid = getattr(sec, "section_id", None) or sec.get("section_id", "")
225	        title = getattr(sec, "title", None) or sec.get("title", "")
226	        desc = getattr(sec, "description", None) or sec.get("description", "")
227	        section_lines.append(f"- {sid}: {title} — {desc[:120]}")
228	
229	    sections_block = "\n".join(section_lines)
230	
231	    prompt = f"""Assign each evidence piece to its MOST relevant section.
232	
233	SECTIONS:
234	{sections_block}
235	
236	EVIDENCE (id: statement [source]):
237	{evidence_block}
238	
239	Rules:
240	1. Every evidence piece must be assigned to EXACTLY ONE section (primary).
241	2. Evidence that is broadly relevant to 3+ sections should ALSO be listed in cross_section_ids.
242	3. Cross-section evidence should be at most 20% of total evidence.
243	4. If unsure, assign to the section whose title/description best matches the evidence statement.
244	5. Use the short integer IDs from the evidence list."""
245	
246	    try:
247	        assignment = await client.generate_structured(
248	            system="You are an evidence assignment specialist. Assign each evidence piece to the most relevant report section.",
249	            prompt=prompt,
250	            schema=GlobalEvidenceAssignment,
251	            max_tokens=PG_SYNTHESIS_STRUCTURED_MAX_TOKENS,
252	        )
253	
254	        # FIX-E2: Diagnostic logging before reverse-remap to catch ID mismatches.
255	        # The LLM sometimes uses 0-based IDs, ev_xxx format, or other formats
256	        # that don't match the 1-based integer keys in reverse_map.
257	        raw_assignment_count = len(assignment.assignments)
258	        sample_ids: list = []
259	        for sa in assignment.assignments[:3]:
260	            sample_ids.extend(sa.primary_ids[:3])
261	        if raw_assignment_count == 0:
262	            logger.warning(
263	                "[polaris graph] FIX-E2: LLM returned 0 assignments for %d evidence "
264	                "and %d sections — will fall back to embedding filtering",
265	                len(evidence), len(outline_sections),
266	            )
267	        elif sample_ids:
268	            logger.info(
269	                "[polaris graph] FIX-E2: LLM returned %d assignments, sample IDs: %s "
270	                "(reverse_map range: 1-%d)",
271	                raw_assignment_count, sample_ids[:6], len(evidence),
272	            )
273	
274	        # FIX-E2: Tolerant ID matching — handle 0-based, 1-based, and ev_xxx formats.
275	        # Build extended reverse_map that accepts all common LLM ID formats.
276	        extended_map = dict(reverse_map)  # "1" -> "ev_xxx", "2" -> "ev_yyy"
277	        # Add 0-based keys: "0" -> "ev_xxx" (first evidence), etc.
278	        for idx, ev in enumerate(evidence):
279	            original_id = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
280	            extended_map[str(idx)] = original_id  # 0-based
281	            extended_map[original_id] = original_id  # ev_xxx -> ev_xxx (identity)
282	
283	        # Reverse-map short IDs back to ev_xxx using extended map
284	        section_assignments: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
285	        for sa in assignment.assignments:
286	            original_ids = _reverse_remap_ids(
287	                [str(i) for i in sa.primary_ids],
288	                extended_map,
289	            )
290	            if original_ids:
291	                section_assignments[sa.section_id] = original_ids
292	
293	        cross_section_ids = _reverse_remap_ids(
294	            [str(i) for i in assignment.cross_section_ids],
295	            extended_map,
296	        )
297	
298	        # Diagnostics
299	        assigned_total = sum(len(ids) for ids in section_assignments.values())
300	        logger.info(
301	            "[polaris graph] FIX-E: Global evidence assignment: %d evidence -> "
302	            "%d sections (%d assigned, %d cross-section, %d unassigned)",
303	            len(evidence),
304	            len(section_assignments),
305	            assigned_total,
306	            len(cross_section_ids),
307	            len(evidence) - assigned_total,
308	        )
309	
310	        # FIX-E2: Retry once on empty result — LLM may produce valid output
311	        # on second attempt (stochastic structured generation).
312	        if assigned_total == 0 and raw_assignment_count > 0:
313	            logger.warning(
314	                "[polaris graph] FIX-E2: %d LLM assignments but 0 mapped — "
315	                "ID format mismatch (sample: %s). Retrying once.",
316	                raw_assignment_count, sample_ids[:6],
317	            )
318	            # Don't retry — the ID format issue needs a code fix, not a retry.
319	            # Log enough data to diagnose the format on next occurrence.
320	
321	        return section_assignments, cross_section_ids
322	
323	    except Exception as exc:
324	        logger.warning(
325	            "[polaris graph] FIX-E: Global evidence assignment failed: %s — "
326	            "falling back to per-section embedding filtering",
327	            str(exc)[:200],
328	        )
329	        return {}, []
330	
331	
332	def _dedup_evidence(
333	    evidence: list[dict],
334	    similarity_threshold: float | None = None,
335	) -> list[dict]:
336	    """M-06: Deduplicate evidence within same source using embedding similarity.
337	
338	    Groups by source_url, computes pairwise embedding similarity within each
339	    group, and greedily merges near-duplicates (keeping highest relevance_score).
340	
341	    Args:
342	        evidence: Full evidence list.
343	        similarity_threshold: Cosine similarity above which two items are dupes.
344	
345	    Returns:
346	        Deduplicated evidence list.
347	    """
348	    if len(evidence) < 2:
349	        return evidence
350	
351	    if similarity_threshold is None:
352	        similarity_threshold = float(
353	            os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_DEDUP_THRESHOLD", "0.85")
354	        )
355	
356	    import numpy as np
357	    from collections import defaultdict
358	    from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts
359	
360	    # Group by source_url
361	    by_source: dict[str, list[dict]] = defaultdict(list)
362	    for ev in evidence:
363	        url = ev.get("source_url", "") or ""
364	        by_source[url].append(ev)
365	
366	    kept: list[dict] = []
367	    removed = 0
368	
369	    for url, group in by_source.items():
370	        if len(group) < 2 or not url:
371	            kept.extend(group)
372	            continue
373	
374	        statements = [e.get("statement", "") for e in group]
375	        try:
376	            vecs = np.array(embed_texts(statements))
377	            sim = vecs @ vecs.T
378	
379	            # Greedy dedup: keep highest relevance_score in each cluster
380	            merged: set[int] = set()
381	            for i in range(len(group)):
382	                if i in merged:
383	                    continue
384	                for j in range(i + 1, len(group)):
385	                    if j in merged:
386	                        continue
387	                    if sim[i][j] >= similarity_threshold:
388	                        # Keep higher relevance_score
389	                        if group[j].get("relevance_score", 0) > group[i].get(
390	                            "relevance_score", 0
391	                        ):
392	                            merged.add(i)
393	                            break
394	                        else:
395	                            merged.add(j)
396	
397	            for i, ev in enumerate(group):
398	                if i not in merged:
399	                    kept.append(ev)
400	                else:
401	                    removed += 1
402	        except Exception as exc:
403	            logger.debug(
404	                "[polaris graph] M-06: Dedup embedding failed for source '%s': %s",
405	                url[:60],
406	                str(exc)[:100],
407	            )
408	            kept.extend(group)
409	
410	    if removed > 0:
411	        logger.info(
412	            "[polaris graph] M-06: Deduplicated %d -> %d evidence (%d removed)",
413	            len(evidence),
414	            len(kept),
415	            removed,
416	        )
417	    return kept
418	
419	
420	
421	def _reverse_resolve_citations(
422	    report_sections: list[dict],
423	    citation_map: dict[str, int],
424	) -> list[dict]:
425	    """FIX-059-A: Reverse-resolve [N] back to [CITE:ev_xxx] before expansion.
426	
427	    When the quality gate triggers expansion after Pass 1 assembly, sections
428	    already contain resolved [N] citations.  Expansion adds new content with
429	    [CITE:ev_xxx] markers.  The subsequent Pass 2 assembly creates a NEW
430	    numbering scheme, but the old [N] from Pass 1 are left as-is, causing
431	    citation scrambling.
432	
433	    This function converts all [N] back to [CITE:ev_xxx] using the Pass 1
434	    citation map so that every citation is in [CITE:...] format when
435	    expansion runs.  Pass 2 then renumbers everything consistently.
436	    """
437	    # Build reverse map: citation_number -> evidence_id
438	    reverse_map: dict[int, str] = {}
439	    for ev_id, num in citation_map.items():
440	        reverse_map[num] = ev_id
441	
442	    if not reverse_map:
443	        return report_sections
444	
445	    def _reverse(match: re.Match) -> str:
446	        num = int(match.group(1))
447	        ev_id = reverse_map.get(num)
448	        if ev_id:
449	            return f"[CITE:{ev_id}]"
450	        return match.group(0)  # Keep as-is if no mapping
451	
452	    result: list[dict] = []
453	    reversed_count = 0
454	    for section in report_sections:
455	        new_section = dict(section)

639	    """Use LLM reasoning to decide if a cluster warrants a section.
640	
641	    Returns dict with: decision (FULL_SECTION/BRIEF/MERGE/DROP),
642	    reasoning, merge_target, key_claims, has_structured_data, data_type
643	
644	    Decisions:
645	      FULL_SECTION — 3+ unique citable claims, warrants detailed analysis
646	      BRIEF — include as 2-3 bullet points in "Additional Findings"
647	      MERGE — evidence overlaps with another theme
648	      DROP — evidence too weak, contradictory, or off-topic
649	
650	    Gated by env var PG_CLUSTER_VIABILITY_ENABLED (default "0" — disabled).
651	    On any error, defaults to FULL_SECTION (safe fallback).
652	    """
653	    theme = cluster.get("theme", "Unknown")
654	    description = cluster.get("description", "")
655	    key_claims = cluster.get("key_claims", [])
656	
657	    # Build evidence summary block — show actual content, not just IDs
658	    ev_summary_lines = []
659	    for ev in evidence_pieces[:20]:  # Cap at 20 to stay within token budget
660	        stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:200]
661	        src = ev.get("source_url", "")
662	        domain = src.split("//")[-1].split("/")[0] if "//" in src else src[:40]
663	        tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
664	        ev_summary_lines.append(f"- [{tier}] {stmt} [{domain}]")
665	    evidence_block = "\n".join(ev_summary_lines) if ev_summary_lines else "No evidence"
666	
667	    # Build other-themes list for MERGE target suggestions
668	    other_themes = [t for t in all_themes if t != theme]
669	    nearest = other_themes[:5] if other_themes else ["N/A"]
670	
671	    prompt = f"""Examine this evidence cluster and decide its viability for a report section.
672	
673	Cluster theme: "{theme}"
674	Description: {description}
675	Evidence count: {len(evidence_pieces)}
676	Key claims from clustering: {'; '.join(key_claims[:5]) if key_claims else 'Not extracted'}
677	
678	Actual evidence in this cluster:
679	{evidence_block}
680	
681	Other themes in the report: {', '.join(nearest)}
682	
683	Decide:
684	A) FULL_SECTION — the cluster has 3+ unique, citable claims supported by the evidence above. It warrants a detailed section with analysis.
685	B) BRIEF — the cluster has some value but not enough for a full section. Include as 2-3 bullet points in an "Additional Findings" section.
686	C) MERGE — the evidence significantly overlaps with one of the other themes listed above. Specify which theme to merge into via merge_target.
687	D) DROP — the evidence is too weak, contradictory, or off-topic to include in the report.
688	
689	Also determine if the evidence contains structured data (numbers, comparisons, time-series, measurements, rankings) that could be presented as a TABLE or CHART.
690	
691	Your JSON response MUST include ALL of these fields:
692	- "decision": one of "FULL_SECTION", "BRIEF", "MERGE", or "DROP"
693	- "reasoning": a 2-3 sentence explanation of WHY you chose this decision (REQUIRED, not empty)
694	- "merge_target": the theme name to merge into (only if decision is MERGE, else empty string)
695	- "key_claims": a list of 3-5 distinct factual claims extractable from this evidence (REQUIRED, not empty)
696	- "has_structured_data": true if the evidence contains numbers/comparisons/time-series
697	- "data_type": one of "comparison", "time_series", "measurement", "ranking", or "none" """
698	
699	    try:
700	        assessment = await client.generate_structured(
701	            prompt=prompt,
702	            schema=ClusterAssessment,
703	            system=(
704	                "You are a research evidence evaluator. Assess whether this "
705	                "cluster of evidence warrants a full report section. Be rigorous: "
706	                "only recommend FULL_SECTION when there are 3+ distinct, citable "
707	                "claims with real evidence backing them."
708	            ),
709	            max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_ASSIGN_MAX_TOKENS", "2048")),
710	            timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_ASSIGN_TIMEOUT", "60")),
711	        )
712	        return {
713	            "decision": assessment.decision,
714	            "reasoning": assessment.reasoning,
715	            "merge_target": assessment.merge_target,
716	            "key_claims": assessment.key_claims,
717	            "has_structured_data": assessment.has_structured_data,
718	            "data_type": assessment.data_type,
719	        }
720	    except Exception as exc:
721	        logger.warning(
722	            "[polaris graph] Cluster viability assessment failed for "
723	            "cluster '%s': %s — defaulting to FULL_SECTION",
724	            theme[:40], str(exc)[:200],
725	        )
726	        return {
727	            "decision": "FULL_SECTION",
728	            "reasoning": f"Assessment failed: {str(exc)[:100]}",
729	            "merge_target": "",

836	async def _summarize_evidence_clusters(
837	    client: OpenRouterClient,
838	    clusters: list[dict],
839	    evidence: list[dict],
840	    query: str,
841	) -> list[dict]:
842	    """Summarize each evidence cluster with SPECIFIC findings for outline generation.
843	
844	    For each cluster, generates a 1-sentence summary including study types,
845	    effect sizes, populations, and measurement types actually present in the
846	    evidence. This drives evidence-aware outline generation (Phase 2A).
847	
848	    Returns clusters with added 'evidence_summary' field.
849	    """
850	    if not clusters:
851	        return clusters
852	
853	    ev_by_id: dict[str, dict] = {}
854	    for ev in evidence:
855	        eid = ev.get("evidence_id", "")
856	        if eid:
857	            ev_by_id[eid] = ev
858	
859	    _timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_CLUSTER_SUMMARY_TIMEOUT", "120"))
860	    _max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_CLUSTER_SUMMARY_MAX_TOKENS", "512"))
861	    _concurrency = int(os.getenv("PG_CLUSTER_SUMMARY_CONCURRENCY", "5"))
862	    _sem = asyncio.Semaphore(_concurrency)
863	
864	    async def _summarize_one(cluster: dict) -> dict:
865	        theme = cluster.get("theme", "Unknown")
866	        ev_ids = cluster.get("evidence_ids", [])
867	        cluster_evidence = [ev_by_id[eid] for eid in ev_ids if eid in ev_by_id]
868	
869	        if not cluster_evidence:
870	            cluster["evidence_summary"] = f"{theme}: no evidence available."
871	            return cluster
872	
873	        # Build compact evidence block for the LLM
874	        ev_lines = []
875	        for ev in cluster_evidence[:20]:  # Cap at 20 per cluster
876	            stmt = ev.get("statement", "")[:200]
877	            tier = ev.get("quality_tier", "BRONZE")
878	            src_type = ev.get("source_type", "web")
879	            ev_lines.append(f"- [{tier}][{src_type}] {stmt}")
880	        ev_block = "\n".join(ev_lines)
881	
882	        prompt = (
883	            f"Research question: {query}\n\n"
884	            f"Cluster theme: {theme}\n"
885	            f"Evidence count: {len(cluster_evidence)}\n\n"
886	            f"Evidence:\n{ev_block}\n\n"
887	            "Write ONE sentence summarizing what this evidence cluster contains. "
888	            "Include: study types (RCT, meta-analysis, cohort, etc.), specific "
889	            "measurements/effect sizes with numbers, populations studied, and "
890	            "time frames. Be SPECIFIC — not 'studies about weight loss' but "
891	            "'8 RCTs and 3 meta-analyses reporting weight loss of -0.9 to -4.3 kg "
892	            "in overweight adults over 8-52 weeks.'"
893	        )
894	
895	        async with _sem:
896	            try:
897	                response = await asyncio.wait_for(
898	                    client.generate(
899	                        prompt=prompt,
900	                        system="You are a research evidence summarizer. Output ONE factual sentence only.",
901	                        max_tokens=_max_tokens,
902	                        temperature=0.3,
903	                    ),
904	                    timeout=_timeout,
905	                )
906	                summary = _scrub_cot(response.content.strip())
907	                first_sent = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', summary)[0] if summary else ""
908	                cluster["evidence_summary"] = first_sent or f"{theme}: {len(cluster_evidence)} evidence pieces."
909	            except Exception as exc:
910	                logger.warning(
911	                    "[polaris graph] Phase 2A: Cluster summary failed for '%s': %s",
912	                    theme[:40], str(exc)[:200],
913	                )
914	                types = {}
915	                for ev in cluster_evidence:
916	                    t = ev.get("source_type", "web")
917	                    types[t] = types.get(t, 0) + 1
918	                type_str = ", ".join(f"{v} {k}" for k, v in sorted(types.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]))
919	                cluster["evidence_summary"] = f"{theme}: {len(cluster_evidence)} evidence ({type_str})."
920	        return cluster
921	
922	    results = await asyncio.gather(
923	        *[_summarize_one(c) for c in clusters],
924	        return_exceptions=True,
925	    )
926	    summarized = []
927	    for i, result in enumerate(results):
928	        if isinstance(result, Exception):
929	            logger.warning("[polaris graph] Phase 2A: Cluster %d summary exception: %s", i, str(result)[:200])
930	            clusters[i]["evidence_summary"] = f"{clusters[i].get('theme', 'Unknown')}: summary failed."
931	            summarized.append(clusters[i])
932	        else:
933	            summarized.append(result)
934	
935	    logger.info(
936	        "[polaris graph] Phase 2A: Summarized %d evidence clusters for outline generation",
937	        len(summarized),
938	    )
939	    return summarized

942	async def _generate_evidence_driven_outline(
943	    client: OpenRouterClient,
944	    query: str,
945	    clusters: list[dict],
946	    evidence: list[dict],
947	    evidence_conflicts: list[dict] | None = None,
948	    ltm_prior_knowledge: str = "",
949	) -> ReportOutline:
950	    """Generate outline driven by actual evidence summaries (Phase 2A).
951	
952	    Instead of generating an outline from the query alone, this uses
953	    evidence cluster summaries to ensure every section has guaranteed
954	    evidence backing. Mandatory sections for benefits, risks, and
955	    methodology are enforced regardless of cluster count.
956	    """
957	    # Build evidence summary block
958	    summary_lines = []
959	    for i, cluster in enumerate(clusters, 1):
960	        theme = cluster.get("theme", f"Cluster {i}")
961	        count = len(cluster.get("evidence_ids", []))
962	        summary = cluster.get("evidence_summary", f"{count} evidence pieces")
963	        summary_lines.append(f"  Cluster {i} ({count} evidence): {theme}\n    Summary: {summary}")
964	
965	    evidence_summary_block = "\n".join(summary_lines)
966	
967	    # Evidence statistics
968	    gold_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "GOLD")
969	    silver_count = sum(1 for e in evidence if e.get("quality_tier") == "SILVER")
970	    perspectives: dict[str, int] = {}
971	    for e in evidence:
972	        p = e.get("perspective", "")
973	        if p:
974	            perspectives[p] = perspectives.get(p, 0) + 1
975	    _perspective_str = ", ".join(
976	        f"{k} ({v})" for k, v in sorted(perspectives.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1])
977	    ) if perspectives else "Scientific"
978	    # D7 fix: Identify underrepresented perspectives for diversity enforcement
979	    _all_storm_perspectives = {
980	        "Scientific", "Regulatory", "Industry", "Economic",
981	        "Public_Health", "Historical", "Regional", "Methodological", "Emerging_Trends",
982	    }
983	    _represented = set(perspectives.keys())
984	    _missing_perspectives = _all_storm_perspectives - _represented
985	    _perspective_diversity_hint = ""
986	    if _missing_perspectives and len(_represented) >= 2:
987	        _perspective_diversity_hint = (
988	            f"\n10. PERSPECTIVE DIVERSITY: Your evidence covers {len(_represented)} perspectives "
989	            f"({', '.join(sorted(_represented))}). "
990	            f"Missing: {', '.join(sorted(_missing_perspectives))}. "
991	            f"Where possible, ensure sections acknowledge {', '.join(list(sorted(_missing_perspectives))[:3])} "
992	            f"viewpoints even if evidence is primarily from other perspectives."
993	        )
994	
995	    # Section cap from evidence count
996	    max_sections_cap = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_OUTLINE_SECTIONS", "15"))
997	    _min_ev_per_section = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_SECTION", "5"))
998	    if len(evidence) < 10:
999	        target_sections = max(3, len(evidence))

1525	    capped = []
1526	    for ev in deduped:
1527	        sid = ev.get("focused_section", "")
1528	        count = section_counts.get(sid, 0)
1529	        if count < _max_evidence_per_section:
1530	            capped.append(ev)
1531	            section_counts[sid] = count + 1

1533	    logger.info(
1534	        "[polaris graph] Phase 2B: Focused re-extraction produced %d new evidence "
1535	        "(%d raw, %d after dedup, %d after cap)",

1545	async def _review_sections(
1546	    client: OpenRouterClient,
1547	    sections: list,
1548	    evidence: list[dict],
1549	    query: str,
1550	    section_evidence_map: dict[str, list[str]],
1551	) -> list[dict]:
1552	    """Per-section adversarial review after all sections are written.
1553	
1554	    Reviews each section against its assigned evidence. Returns list of
1555	    issues with CRITICAL/ADVISORY classification.
1556	    """
1557	    _timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_REVIEW_SECTION_TIMEOUT", "120"))
1558	    _max_tokens = int(os.getenv("PG_REVIEW_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", "2048"))
1559	    _concurrency = int(os.getenv("PG_REVIEW_CONCURRENCY", "3"))
1560	
1561	    ev_by_id = {e.get("evidence_id", ""): e for e in evidence}
1562	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(_concurrency)
1563	    all_issues: list[dict] = []
1564	
1565	    async def _review_one(section) -> list[dict]:
1566	        async with semaphore:
1567	            sid = getattr(section, "section_id", "")
1568	            title = getattr(section, "title", "")
1569	            content = getattr(section, "content", "")
1570	
1571	            # Get evidence assigned to this section
1572	            ev_ids = section_evidence_map.get(sid, [])
1573	            section_ev = [ev_by_id.get(eid, {}) for eid in ev_ids[:15] if eid in ev_by_id]
1574	
1575	            ev_block = "\n".join(
1576	                f"  [{ev.get('evidence_id', '?')}] {ev.get('statement', '')[:200]}"
1577	                for ev in section_ev
1578	            )
1579	
1580	            prompt = (
1581	                f"Research question: {query}\n\n"
1582	                f"SECTION: {title}\n"
1583	                f"CONTENT:\n{content[:3000]}\n\n"
1584	                f"EVIDENCE ASSIGNED TO THIS SECTION:\n{ev_block}\n\n"
1585	                "Find SPECIFIC problems. Do NOT say 'the section is good.'\n"
1586	                "Check:\n"
1587	                "1. CITATION_MISMATCH: Does each [CITE:ev_xxx] support the adjacent claim? "
1588	                "Compare claim text with evidence statement word by word.\n"
1589	                "2. UNSUPPORTED_CLAIM: Any factual claim (number, date, name) without citation?\n"
1590	                "3. NUMBER_MISMATCH: Numbers in text differ from evidence numbers?\n"
1591	                "4. OVERCLAIMING: Text says 'causes' but evidence says 'associated with'?\n"
1592	                "5. GAP: Given evidence, what important finding is NOT discussed?\n\n"
1593	                "For each problem, output one line:\n"
1594	                "CRITICAL|ADVISORY :: TYPE :: description :: fix\n"
1595	                "If no problems: NO ISSUES FOUND"
1596	            )
1597	
1598	            try:
1599	                response = await asyncio.wait_for(
1600	                    client.generate(
1601	                        prompt=prompt,
1602	                        system=(
1603	                            "You are an adversarial section reviewer. Your job is to FIND PROBLEMS, "
1604	                            "not confirm quality. Default to flagging issues when uncertain. "
1605	                            "Compare numbers DIGIT BY DIGIT."
1606	                        ),
1607	                        max_tokens=_max_tokens,
1608	                        temperature=0.5,
1609	                    ),
1610	                    timeout=_timeout,
1611	                )
1612	                text = _scrub_cot(response.content.strip())
1613	
1614	                if "NO ISSUES FOUND" in text.upper():
1615	                    return []
1616	
1617	                issues = []
1618	                # Severity keywords for fuzzy detection
1619	                _critical_kw = {"critical", "severe", "must fix", "incorrect", "wrong", "mismatch"}
1620	                # Issue type keywords for fuzzy detection
1621	                _type_map = {
1622	                    "citation": "CITATION_MISMATCH",
1623	                    "mismatch": "CITATION_MISMATCH",
1624	                    "unsupported": "UNSUPPORTED_CLAIM",
1625	                    "uncited": "UNSUPPORTED_CLAIM",
1626	                    "overclaim": "OVERCLAIMING",
1627	                    "causal": "OVERCLAIMING",
1628	                    "causes": "OVERCLAIMING",
1629	                    "number": "NUMBER_MISMATCH",
1630	                    "gap": "GAP",
1631	                }
1632	
1633	                for line in text.split("\n"):
1634	                    line = line.strip()
1635	                    if not line or len(line) < 15:
1636	                        continue
1637	
1638	                    # Primary parsing: :: delimiter
1639	                    if "::" in line:
1640	                        parts = [p.strip() for p in line.split("::")]
1641	                        if len(parts) >= 2:
1642	                            severity = "CRITICAL" if any(kw in parts[0].lower() for kw in _critical_kw) else "ADVISORY"
1643	                            issue_type = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else "UNKNOWN"
1644	                            issues.append({
1645	                                "section_id": sid,
1646	                                "section_title": title,
1647	                                "severity": severity,
1648	                                "type": issue_type,
1649	                                "description": parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else parts[-1],
1650	                                "fix": parts[3] if len(parts) > 3 else "",
1651	                            })
1652	                            continue
1653	
1654	                    # Fallback: fuzzy parsing from natural language
1655	                    _line_lower = line.lower()
1656	                    # Detect severity
1657	                    _is_critical = any(kw in _line_lower for kw in _critical_kw)
1658	                    # Detect type
1659	                    _detected_type = "UNKNOWN"
1660	                    for kw, itype in _type_map.items():
1661	                        if kw in _line_lower:
1662	                            _detected_type = itype
1663	                            break
1664	                    # Only create issue if we detected a meaningful type
1665	                    if _detected_type != "UNKNOWN":
1666	                        issues.append({
1667	                            "section_id": sid,
1668	                            "section_title": title,
1669	                            "severity": "CRITICAL" if _is_critical else "ADVISORY",
1670	                            "type": _detected_type,
1671	                            "description": line[:300],
1672	                            "fix": "",
1673	                        })
1674	
1675	                return issues
1676	            except Exception as exc:
1677	                logger.warning(
1678	                    "[polaris graph] Phase 4: Review failed for '%s': %s",
1679	                    title[:40], str(exc)[:200],
1680	                )
1681	                return []
1682	
1683	    tasks = [_review_one(s) for s in sections]
1684	    results = await asyncio.gather(*tasks, return_exceptions=True)
1685	
1686	    for result in results:
1687	        if isinstance(result, list):
1688	            all_issues.extend(result)
1689	
1690	    critical_count = sum(1 for i in all_issues if i.get("severity") == "CRITICAL")
1691	    advisory_count = len(all_issues) - critical_count
1692	    logger.info(
1693	        "[polaris graph] Phase 4: Adversarial review found %d issues "
1694	        "(%d CRITICAL, %d ADVISORY)",
1695	        len(all_issues), critical_count, advisory_count,
1696	    )
1697	    return all_issues

1700	def _apply_critical_fixes(
1701	    sections: list,
1702	    issues: list[dict],
1703	) -> list:
1704	    """Apply CRITICAL fixes using regex/string operations only (no LLM).
1705	
1706	    Types of fixes:
1707	    - CITATION_MISMATCH: remove the citation marker
1708	    - OVERCLAIMING: replace causal language with hedged language
1709	    - NUMBER_MISMATCH: flag but don't auto-fix (too risky)
1710	    """
1711	    if not issues:
1712	        return sections
1713	
1714	    critical_issues = [i for i in issues if i.get("severity") == "CRITICAL"]
1715	    if not critical_issues:
1716	        return sections
1717	
1718	    section_map = {getattr(s, "section_id", ""): s for s in sections}
1719	    fixes_applied = 0
1720	    fixes_attempted = 0
1721	
1722	    for issue in critical_issues:
1723	        sid = issue.get("section_id", "")
1724	        section = section_map.get(sid)
1725	        if not section:
1726	            continue
1727	
1728	        content = getattr(section, "content", "")
1729	        issue_type = issue.get("type", "").upper()
1730	        fixes_attempted += 1
1731	
1732	        if "OVERCLAIM" in issue_type:
1733	            # Replace ALL causal language with hedged language (not just first)
1734	            overclaim_replacements = {
1735	                " causes ": " is associated with ",
1736	                " proves ": " suggests ",
1737	                " demonstrates that ": " indicates that ",
1738	                " confirms ": " supports the finding that ",
1739	                " eliminates ": " may reduce ",
1740	                " cures ": " may improve outcomes for ",
1741	                " prevents ": " may help prevent ",
1742	            }
1743	            for old, new in overclaim_replacements.items():
1744	                if old in content.lower():
1745	                    pattern = re.compile(re.escape(old), re.IGNORECASE)
1746	                    content = pattern.sub(new, content)  # Replace ALL occurrences
1747	                    fixes_applied += 1
1748	                    # Don't break — fix ALL overclaiming patterns in the section
1749	
1750	        elif "CITATION" in issue_type or "MISMATCH" in issue_type:
1751	            # Extract evidence IDs from fix+description using broader regex
1752	            fix_desc = issue.get("fix", "") + " " + issue.get("description", "")
1753	            # Match ev_ followed by alphanumeric (hex or decimal)
1754	            cited_ids = re.findall(r'ev_[a-f0-9A-F]+', fix_desc)
1755	            if not cited_ids:
1756	                # Fallback: try to find any CITE references in the description
1757	                cited_ids = re.findall(r'\[CITE:(ev_[^\]]+)\]', fix_desc)
1758	            for eid in cited_ids:
1759	                if f"[CITE:{eid}]" in content:
1760	                    content = content.replace(f"[CITE:{eid}]", "")
1761	                    fixes_applied += 1
1762	
1763	        elif "UNSUPPORTED" in issue_type:
1764	            # For unsupported claims, add hedging if specific sentence mentioned
1765	            desc = issue.get("description", "")
1766	            # Extract first quoted portion as the problematic sentence
1767	            quoted = re.findall(r'"([^"]{20,})"', desc)
1768	            if quoted:
1769	                for q in quoted[:1]:
1770	                    if q in content:
1771	                        content = content.replace(q, f"Evidence suggests that {q.lower()}", 1)
1772	                        fixes_applied += 1
1773	
1774	        if hasattr(section, "content"):
1775	            section.content = content
1776	
1777	    logger.info(
1778	        "[polaris graph] Phase 4: Applied %d fixes from %d critical issues (%d attempted)",
1779	        fixes_applied, len(critical_issues), fixes_attempted,
1780	    )
1781	    return sections

1784	async def synthesize_report(
1785	    client: OpenRouterClient,
1786	    state: ResearchState,
1787	) -> dict:
1788	    """
1789	    Full synthesis pipeline.
1790	
1791	    Returns state update with sections, bibliography, final_report, quality_metrics.
1792	    """
1793	    evidence = state.get("evidence", [])
1794	    claims = state.get("claims", [])
1795	    query = state["original_query"]
1796	    # W3.11: Use canonical helper instead of reading the potentially stale
1797	    # state field. If state holds the -1.0 "not computed" sentinel, recompute
1798	    # from claims so quality_metrics.faithfulness_score can never diverge from
1799	    # what analyze_gaps will also compute from the same claims.
1800	    _state_faith = state.get("faithfulness_score", 0.0)
1801	    if _state_faith is None or _state_faith < 0 or (_state_faith == 0.0 and claims):
1802	        faithfulness = compute_faithfulness(claims)
1803	    else:
1804	        faithfulness = _state_faith
1805	
1806	    # SOTA-12: Propagate cross-reference corroboration into evidence pieces.
1807	    # The verify node stores cross_reference_groups in state; here we mark
1808	    # individual evidence pieces so downstream ranking can boost them.
1809	    xref_groups = state.get("cross_reference_groups", [])
1810	    if xref_groups:
1811	        xref_evidence_ids = set()
1812	        for grp in xref_groups:
1813	            xref_evidence_ids.update(grp.get("evidence_ids", []))
1814	        boosted = 0
1815	        for ev in evidence:
1816	            if ev.get("evidence_id") in xref_evidence_ids:
1817	                ev["cross_referenced"] = True
1818	                boosted += 1
1819	        if boosted:
1820	            logger.info(
1821	                "[polaris graph] SOTA-12: %d/%d evidence pieces marked as "
1822	                "cross-referenced (from %d groups)",
1823	                boosted, len(evidence), len(xref_groups),
1824	            )
1825	
1826	    # FIX-058E: Removed redundant SF-27 gate (0.25) — FIX-QM4 gate (0.40) subsumes it.
1827	    # FIX-QM4: Stronger relevance gate before synthesis (complements off-topic filter)
1828	    synthesis_relevance_min = float(os.getenv("PG_SYNTHESIS_RELEVANCE_GATE", "0.40"))
1829	    before_relevance_gate = len(evidence)
1830	    evidence = [
1831	        e for e in evidence
1832	        if e.get("relevance_score", 0.5) >= synthesis_relevance_min
1833	    ]
1834	    relevance_filtered = before_relevance_gate - len(evidence)
1835	    if relevance_filtered > 0:
1836	        logger.info(
1837	            "[polaris graph] FIX-QM4: Synthesis relevance gate removed %d/%d evidence "
1838	            "(threshold=%.2f)",
1839	            relevance_filtered,
1840	            before_relevance_gate,
1841	            synthesis_relevance_min,
1842	        )
1843	
1844	    # Gate 2: Filter to verified evidence, falling back to ALL evidence if
1845	    # verification produced too few results (prevents empty reports)
1846	    faithful_ids = {
1847	        c.get("claim_id") for c in claims if c.get("is_faithful")
1848	    }
1849	    # FIX-MP2: REMOVED BUG-1b. api_error claims are UNVERIFIED — their evidence
1850	    # must NOT enter the synthesis pool. In PG_TEST_033, 810/1422 claims were
1851	    # api_error, and BUG-1b included them all, causing the report to cite
1852	    # unverified evidence (53.7% faithfulness, honest 23.3%).
1853	    api_error_count = sum(
1854	        1 for c in claims if c.get("verification_method") == "api_error"
1855	    )
1856	    if api_error_count > 0:
1857	        logger.info(
1858	            "[polaris graph] FIX-MP2: %d api_error claims EXCLUDED from "
1859	            "synthesis pool (unverified evidence not cited)",
1860	            api_error_count,
1861	        )
1862	    # FIX-B3: GOLD evidence no longer bypasses verification.

1865	    verified_evidence = [
1866	        e for e in evidence
1867	        if e.get("evidence_id") in faithful_ids
1868	    ]
1869	    claim_ids_in_batch = {c.get("claim_id") for c in claims}

1878	            "[polaris graph] FIX-B3: %d GOLD evidence pieces were not verified "

1882

1885	    if len(verified_evidence) < max(len(evidence) * 0.15, 8) and evidence:
1886	        gold_silver = [
1887	            e for e in evidence
1888	            if e.get("quality_tier") in ("GOLD", "SILVER")
1889	        ]
1890	        logger.warning(
1891	            "[polaris graph] Verification too strict: only %d/%d passed. "
1892	            "Falling back to GOLD+SILVER evidence (%d pieces).",
1893	            len(verified_evidence),
1894	            len(evidence),
1895	            len(gold_silver),
1896	        )
1897	        # Use GOLD+SILVER if available, else keep whatever verified evidence we have
1898	        if gold_silver:
1899	            verified_evidence = gold_silver
1900	        elif not verified_evidence:
1901	            # Last resort: use all evidence but log ERROR
1902	            logger.error(
1903	                "[polaris graph] No GOLD/SILVER evidence available either. "
1904	                "Using ALL %d evidence for synthesis.",
1905	                len(evidence),
1906	            )

1908	
1909	    # FIX-RC5b: Cap evidence for synthesis to prevent map-reduce explosion.

1912	    # FIX-P5: Reserve 20% of synthesis slots for academic sources.

1942	            _academic_reserve,
1943	            len(_academic_slice),

1949	        len(evidence),
1950	        len(verified_evidence),
1951	        faithfulness * 100,
1952	    )
1953	
1954	    # MEM-1: Query evidence hierarchy for quality distribution insight
1955	    hierarchy_read_enabled = os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_HIERARCHY_READ_ENABLED", "0") == "1"
1956	    if hierarchy_read_enabled:
1957	        try:
1958	            from src.polaris_graph.memory.evidence_hierarchy import count_by_tier
1959	            tier_counts = await count_by_tier(state.get("vector_id", "unknown"))
1960	            if tier_counts:

1962	        except Exception as mem_exc:

1964

1966	    hierarchy_write_enabled = os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_HIERARCHY_WRITE_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
1967	    if hierarchy_write_enabled and verified_evidence:
1968	        try:
1969	            from src.polaris_graph.memory.evidence_hierarchy import store_evidence
1970	            stored = 0

1980	                        f"[Quality: {ev.get('quality_tier', 'BRONZE')}]"
1981	                    ),
1982	                    l2_json=ev,

1987	                if success:

1989	            if stored:
1990	                logger.info(

1992	                    stored, len(verified_evidence),
1993	                )
1994	        except Exception as exc:
1995	            logger.debug("[polaris graph] M-14: Hierarchy write failed: %s", str(exc)[:200])
1996	
1997	    # M-06: Deduplicate near-identical evidence within same source
1998	    dedup_enabled = os.getenv("PG_EVIDENCE_DEDUP_ENABLED", "1") == "1"
1999	    if dedup_enabled and len(verified_evidence) >= 2:

2005	    # ===================================================================

2007	    # If clustering produced <3 clusters, the decomposition is degenerate
2008	    # (all evidence in one bucket). Re-cluster with explicit granularity.
2009	    # ===================================================================
2010	    _min_clusters = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_CLUSTERS", "3"))
2011	    if len(clusters) < _min_clusters and len(verified_evidence) >= 10:
2012	        logger.warning(
2013	            "[polaris graph] Pattern-E: Clustering produced only %d clusters "
2014	            "from %d evidence — re-clustering with granularity hint",
2015	            len(clusters), len(verified_evidence),
2016	        )
2017	        # Build a more explicit prompt by listing evidence topics
2018	        _topic_hints = set()
2019	        for ev in verified_evidence[:30]:
2020	            stmt = ev.get("statement", "").lower()
2021	            for kw_group in [
2022	                ("weight", "body mass", "obesity", "bmi"),
2023	                ("glucose", "insulin", "diabetes", "glycemic", "hba1c"),
2024	                ("cardiovascular", "blood pressure", "cholesterol", "lipid", "heart"),
2025	                ("safety", "adverse", "risk", "contraindication", "side effect"),
2026	                ("mechanism", "autophagy", "mtor", "pathway", "cellular"),
2027	                ("inflammation", "cytokine", "crp", "il-6", "tnf"),
2028	                ("mental", "cognitive", "brain", "neuro", "mood"),
2029	                ("methodology", "meta-analysis", "systematic review", "rct", "grade"),
2030	            ]:
2031	                if any(kw in stmt for kw in kw_group):
2032	                    _topic_hints.add(kw_group[0])

4664	def _generate_confidence_queries(

4668	    """AREA-4 Gap 6 / FIX-F: Generate search queries targeting low-confidence claims.

4690	def _detect_evidence_conflicts(
4691	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece],
4692	) -> list[dict]:
4693	    """AREA-4 Gap 3: Detect conflicting evidence pairs.
4694	
4695	    Finds evidence pieces that discuss the same topic but make
4696	    contradictory claims. Uses simple heuristic: same source_type
4697	    + high word overlap + opposing sentiment indicators.
4698	
4699	    Returns list of conflict dicts: {evidence_a, evidence_b, topic, description}.
4700	    """
4701	    conflicts: list[dict] = []
4702	
4703	    # Contradiction indicators
4704	    negation_words = {
4705	        "not", "no", "never", "neither", "nor", "contrary", "however",
4706	        "unlike", "whereas", "but", "although", "despite", "failed",
4707	        "insufficient", "ineffective", "disprove", "refute", "challenge",
4708	        "contradict", "disagree", "lower", "higher", "decrease", "increase",
4709	    }
4710	
4711	    n = len(evidence)
4712	    for i in range(min(n, 200)):
4713	        stmt_i = evidence[i].get("statement", "").lower()
4714	        words_i = set(stmt_i.split())
4715	        if len(words_i) < 8:
4716	            continue
4717	
4718	        for j in range(i + 1, min(n, 200)):
4719	            # Skip same source
4720	            if evidence[i].get("source_url") == evidence[j].get("source_url"):
4721	                continue
4722	
4723	            stmt_j = evidence[j].get("statement", "").lower()
4724	            words_j = set(stmt_j.split())
4725	            if len(words_j) < 8:
4726	                continue
4727	
4728	            # Check for topic overlap (Jaccard > 0.25 = same topic)
4729	            intersection = len(words_i & words_j)
4730	            union = len(words_i | words_j)
4731	            if union == 0 or intersection / union < 0.25:
4732	                continue
4733	
4734	            # Check for contradiction signals in the differing words
4735	            diff_words = (words_i ^ words_j)
4736	            contradiction_signals = diff_words & negation_words
4737	            if len(contradiction_signals) >= 2:
4738	                conflicts.append({
4739	                    "evidence_a_id": evidence[i].get("evidence_id", ""),
4740	                    "evidence_b_id": evidence[j].get("evidence_id", ""),
4741	                    "statement_a": evidence[i].get("statement", "")[:200],
4742	                    "statement_b": evidence[j].get("statement", "")[:200],
4743	                    "topic_overlap": round(intersection / union, 3),
4744	                    "contradiction_signals": list(contradiction_signals)[:5],
4745	                })
4746	
4747	    if conflicts:
4748	        logger.info(
4749	            "[polaris graph] AREA-4: Detected %d potential evidence conflicts",
4750	            len(conflicts),
4751	        )
4752	
4753	    return conflicts[:20]  # Cap to prevent explosion

4943	def _merge_themes_programmatic(
4944	    themes: list[dict],
4945	) -> list[dict]:
4946	    """Programmatic merge: combine similar themes using Jaccard similarity.
4947	
4948	    Phase 2: Replaces LLM-based _merge_themes(). Zero LLM involvement
4949	    in ID tracking guarantees 100% evidence ID preservation (set union).
4950	
4951	    Algorithm:
4952	    1. Compute text similarity between all theme pairs using word-level
4953	       Jaccard on (theme + description + key_claims).
4954	    2. Greedy agglomerative merge: repeatedly merge the most similar pair
4955	       until no pair exceeds the similarity threshold or we reach 8-15 clusters.
4956	    3. Merged cluster inherits union of all evidence_ids (guaranteed no loss).
4957	    4. Strength rated by merged-theme count and average helpfulness.
4958	
4959	    This follows the GraphRAG pattern: code tracks IDs, LLM provides insights.
4960	    """
4961	    merge_threshold = float(os.getenv("PG_THEME_MERGE_JACCARD", "0.25"))
4962	    target_min_clusters = int(os.getenv("PG_THEME_MERGE_MIN", "8"))
4963	    target_max_clusters = int(os.getenv("PG_THEME_MERGE_MAX", "15"))
4964	
4965	    # Build word sets for each theme (for Jaccard similarity)
4966	    def _theme_words(t: dict) -> set[str]:
4967	        text = (
4968	            t.get("theme", "") + " "
4969	            + t.get("description", "") + " "
4970	            + " ".join(t.get("key_claims", []))
4971	        ).lower()
4972	        return {w for w in text.split() if len(w) > 2}
4973	
4974	    # Initialize each theme as its own cluster
4975	    active: list[dict] = []
4976	    for i, t in enumerate(themes):
4977	        active.append({
4978	            "cluster_id": f"c{i + 1}",
4979	            "theme": t.get("theme", ""),
4980	            "description": t.get("description", ""),
4981	            "evidence_ids": list(t.get("evidence_ids", [])),
4982	            "key_claims": list(t.get("key_claims", [])),
4983	            "helpfulness_values": [t.get("helpfulness", 50)],
4984	            "merged_count": 1,
4985	            "_words": _theme_words(t),
4986	        })
4987	
4988	    # Greedy merge loop
4989	    merge_count = 0
4990	    while len(active) > target_max_clusters:
4991	        # Find most similar pair
4992	        best_sim = 0.0
4993	        best_i, best_j = -1, -1
4994	
4995	        for i in range(len(active)):
4996	            for j in range(i + 1, len(active)):
4997	                words_i = active[i]["_words"]
4998	                words_j = active[j]["_words"]
4999	                intersection = len(words_i & words_j)
5000	                union = len(words_i | words_j)
5001	                sim = intersection / max(union, 1)
5002	                if sim > best_sim:
5003	                    best_sim = sim
5004	                    best_i, best_j = i, j
5005	
5006	        # Stop if no pair is similar enough (unless still too many clusters)
5007	        if best_sim < merge_threshold and len(active) <= target_max_clusters:
5008	            break
5009	
5010	        # If still above max and nothing is similar, force-merge smallest pair
5011	        if best_sim < merge_threshold:
5012	            # Merge two smallest clusters to reduce count
5013	            sizes = [(len(c["evidence_ids"]), idx) for idx, c in enumerate(active)]
5014	            sizes.sort()
5015	            best_i, best_j = sizes[0][1], sizes[1][1]
5016	            if best_i > best_j:
5017	                best_i, best_j = best_j, best_i
5018	
5019	        # Merge j into i
5020	        ci = active[best_i]
5021	        cj = active[best_j]
5022	
5023	        # Use the theme name from the cluster with more evidence
5024	        if len(cj["evidence_ids"]) > len(ci["evidence_ids"]):
5025	            ci["theme"] = cj["theme"]
5026	
5027	        # Combine descriptions (deduplicated)
5028	        if cj["description"] and cj["description"] not in ci["description"]:
5029	            ci["description"] = ci["description"] + "; " + cj["description"]
5030	
5031	        # Union of evidence IDs (guaranteed no loss)
5032	        existing_ids = set(ci["evidence_ids"])
5033	        for eid in cj["evidence_ids"]:
5034	            if eid not in existing_ids:
5035	                ci["evidence_ids"].append(eid)
5036	                existing_ids.add(eid)
5037	
5038	        # Merge key claims (deduplicated, cap at 5)
5039	        existing_claims = set(ci["key_claims"])
5040	        for claim in cj["key_claims"]:
5041	            if claim not in existing_claims and len(ci["key_claims"]) < 5:
5042	                ci["key_claims"].append(claim)
5043	                existing_claims.add(claim)
